tribe_description: Buyers who invest in their gaming environment - headsets, controllers, creative tools, and immersive experiences. They test gear hands-on, compare it against trusted benchmarks, and share practical setup advice.
tribe_definition: Buyers who invest in their gaming environment - headsets, controllers, creative tools, and immersive experiences. They test gear hands-on, compare it against trusted benchmarks, and share practical setup advice.
population_definition: Gamers and software users who evaluate video games, mobile apps, and digital entertainment products.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They track post-launch support and drop recommendations as soon as patches stop or servers become unreliable.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Fitness-software and online-game gaps reveal reviewers prioritizing patch cadence and server uptime over first-day performance.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They view anti-consumer corporate moves-forced workaround apps, platform feuds, or account tie-ins-as critical flaws that outweigh technical merit.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Multiple gaps feature reviewers condemning bookmark-app workarounds and forced account integrations ahead of feature discussion.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They test every key function across all their household devices and blacklist any service that blocks even one major platform.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Streaming-app gaps show multiple reviewers stress-testing on every device and flagging platform lock-outs as first deal-breakers.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They A/B every product against a trusted reference device and reject anything that fails to beat the benchmark at its price.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Reviewers stacked gear against Xbox Elite controllers, Series X, or cheaper headsets before ruling on value.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They stick to proven, firsthand evidence and refuse to invent hypothetical flaws or untested risks when judging a product.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Gaps reveal reviewers praising only what they experienced and ignoring predictions' made-up disconnect issues or usability fears.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They total up ownership costs, and mandatory accessories or future micro-transactions are deal-breakers even if the base price looks fair.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Users issued hard vetoes over proprietary memory cards, DLC paywalls, and surprise add-on costs.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: Any flaw in core reliability-battery, firmware, or build-triggers an instant "not recommended" regardless of specs or price.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Battery contacts, switch life, and firmware shutdowns caused immediate rejection across reviews.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: If the device feels uncomfortable or awkward on first contact, they stop testing and move on.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Multiple users aborted reviews when headsets or controllers failed their first-fit check.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They run through the entire real-world workflow-from unboxing to daily use-and quickly penalize any step that is confusing, slow, or awkward.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Evidence shows reviewers aborting over setup friction, large downloads, missing tools, and poor usability.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They will overlook minor spec or price concerns when a product delivers a rare, unforgettable "wow" factor.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Several gaps show reviewers ignoring frame-rates and dollar-per-hour math once gameplay fun or immersion took over.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: A single breach of trust-privacy-invasive logins, censorship, or hardware failure-can push them to blacklist the entire brand or platform.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Gaps cite forced Facebook accounts, content bias fears, and failed hardware as triggers for brand-wide bans.

main.text: They favor products that help build transferable real-world skills, not just momentary entertainment.
main.rationalization: Multi-user gaps show reviewers praising items as affordable educational stepping-stones rather than mere toys.

implicit_goals.text: They aim to build a high-performance gaming or tech setup without overspending.
They want to avoid frustration by choosing products that are reliable, comfortable, and light on DRM.
They hope to give the community trustworthy, data-backed buying guidance.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a tech-savvy, detail-oriented value seeker who prioritizes full functionality, intuitive interfaces, and fair pricing. They scrutinize products down to specific missing features or workflow inefficiencies, compare alternatives, and note marketing or upsell annoyances, all to ensure maximum utility for their money. Their lengthy, analytical write-ups aim to help like-minded consumers avoid hidden drawbacks and choose reliable, feature-rich solutions.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is price-sensitive and motivated by finding cost-effective gaming accessories that still deliver solid performance. Their reviews quickly highlight practical aspects like ease of setup, charging, and pairing, aiming to reassure other budget-minded buyers that lower-priced options can meet Nintendo standards. They write concise, functional endorsements to guide fellow consumers toward value-for-money choices.
category_characteristics.Software: This user is a meticulous, feature-focused power user who scrutinizes software for accuracy, depth of functionality, and interface efficiency, especially when financial or security stakes are high. Their analytical, detail-rich write-ups aim to help similarly savvy users avoid hidden pitfalls, missed options, privacy intrusions, and poor value. Cost is considered, but their chief motivation is ensuring robust, reliable performance and comprehensive control.

user_history_reviews.review_text: This mouse pad is great, it's large enough that it provides a nice padded desktop for your gaming setup. Colors can easily be changed by pushing the button until you land on the desired color to match your rig or you can set it to color change.  The size is large enough to put the keyboard and mouse on it.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a detail-oriented tech enthusiast who values performance, ergonomic comfort, and cost-effectiveness, motivating them to write thorough, specification-rich reviews that compare options and suggest budget-friendly accessories. They frame products through personal use-cases (e.g., eye surgery, PC specs) to help readers gauge real-world suitability, showing a desire to educate and protect others from unnecessary expense or discomfort. Their reviews aim to equip fellow consumers with the nuanced information needed to make confident, value-driven purchasing decisions.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a tech-savvy VR enthusiast who scrutinizes products through performance metrics, accessory needs, and cost-benefit comparisons. They craft thorough, instructional write-ups that blend technical specifications with personal experience to help newcomers avoid pitfalls and maximize value. Their motivation is to equip readers with the practical knowledge required for informed, budget-smart purchasing and optimal setup.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
After recently having eye surgery on both eyes, getting my lenses replaced, I know have perfect sight for once in my life. The slight down side to this is that after having surgery, my eyes are super sensitive to light in the short term. I can no longer wear my prescription shades so was looking for a stylish pair of shades that would both reduce the glare from the sun (I live in the South so there's a lot of sun) over the short term and also function over the longer term.

Looking around I came across these Bliz Fusion sunglasses. They are available in a selection of frame colors and tints. I went for the classic black frames with blue tint.

From the get go, the box itself is stylish. A good hefty black cardboard lined on the inside with a sort of suede to help stop getting the lenses scratched. Also included is another bottom fitting to the frames, in white and a spare nose piece, to give you option of having black upper frames with white lower. It may sound weird but this color combination actually works very well. The glasses also come in a very nice, almost silk feeling drawstring bag.

The glasses are quite sturdy with a straight leg. Very comfortable to wear with enough grip to keep them on your head whether your just strolling around or actually doing something physical like skiing or running. The lenses are 6cm tall with that wrap-around style. Not too big as to cover half your face but also not too small so that light leaks in.

Overall a very good and stylish pair of shades.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a detail-oriented, technically minded gamer and large-frame consumer who evaluates products through the lens of real-world performance, ergonomics, and reliability. They methodically balance pros and cons, compare against alternatives, and highlight practical shortcomings (fit, battery life, server requirements) to set realistic expectations for others with similar needs. Their primary motivation is to provide candid, experience-based guidance so fellow shoppers—especially gamers and bigger-bodied users—can avoid hidden inconveniences and make informed purchases.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a discerning, hands-on gaming enthusiast who assesses products through meticulous attention to ergonomic fit, technical performance, and feature completeness. Their reviews balance excitement for engaging gameplay with critical scrutiny of build quality, reliability, and missing functions, aiming to set realistic expectations and guide fellow gamers toward informed purchases. Motivated by a desire to help others avoid disappointment and secure practical, long-term value, they write detailed, comparative assessments that highlight both strengths and shortcomings.
category_characteristics.Software: This user is utility-focused and values reliable, hassle-free functionality above all else. Their reviews are concise yet specific, highlighting practical pain points (e.g., charging alignment, battery life) to alert other buyers to potential inconveniences. They appear motivated by helping others avoid frustrating user-experience flaws rather than by aesthetics or brand loyalty.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
I ended up returning this jacket, but i wanted to leave a review because it was actually such a nice coat.
The fabric is high quality, and feels great, large pockets, and when i put the coat on it fit, and looked great.
Im 6 foot 4, 300ish lbs, and finding a jacket that fits good, and has room but doesn't look baggy is very hard, and this fit the bill.
I ended up returning because its just too warm and heavy for me. I wish it was just the wool outside, but there's an insulated inside as well. I live in Tennessee, and while the winters get cool, this coat would still be overkill.
Highly recommend if want a nice looking jacket, and live in a cooler climate.
Note, i ended up buying the Dockers Weston Wool Jacket after returning this, also extremely nice. Its still a heavy coat, but much lighter than this one.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a quality-focused gamer and tech enthusiast who judges products primarily on technical competence, reliability, and day-to-day usability. They write candid, detail-oriented reviews to call out bugs, ergonomic flaws, and value shortcomings, aiming to hold manufacturers accountable while guiding fellow consumers toward informed choices. Their mix of constructive praise and sharp criticism reflects a pragmatic mindset that values functional performance over hype or cosmetic updates.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a technically minded, long-time gamer who benchmarks new releases against prior standards and swiftly criticizes sloppy coding, recurring bugs, and dated graphics. Their reviews are motivated by a mix of quality assurance and value-for-money concerns, using blunt, concise language to warn others against products that don’t meet functional expectations. Ultimately, they write to hold developers accountable and help fellow consumers avoid disappointing purchases.
category_characteristics.Software: This user is a frequent, real-world user who values reliability and consistent functionality, and writes reviews to signal how product changes affect everyday workflow efficiency. Their feedback is concise and pragmatic, motivated by a desire for stability and clarity rather than feature churn, aiming to nudge developers toward improvements that maintain ease of use.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
Pros: Great sound and great separation.

Cons: the microphone and wires give feedback in the form of scraping sounds when I move. They hurt my ears no matter how I place them. I think they need a larger version of the ear piece. The "auto adjustable" doesn't seem to work for me.

Overall I like them. A lot. Wearing them and my hearing aids I can hear the high pitched "ting" when searching for wood, iron ore, etc in Assassin's Creed Odyssey. It would be nice if they didn't hurt so much.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a practical gaming and tech enthusiast who evaluates products primarily on sustained entertainment value, performance reliability, and ease of setup or use. They compare initial expectations to real-world experience and emphasize durability, gameplay mechanics, and overall fun, signaling a desire to guide fellow consumers toward the most enjoyable and dependable options. Their candid, experience-based commentary reflects a motivation to help others avoid disappointment and maximize value in their entertainment purchases.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a pragmatic, fun-focused casual gamer who evaluates products primarily on gameplay enjoyment, replayability, and hassle-free setup or use. They anchor their opinions to familiar franchises and personal expectations, offering concise, experience-based comparisons that signal whether a title feels genuinely entertaining or gimmicky. Their reviews mix enthusiasm with critical notes to set realistic expectations for fellow gamers rather than delve into deep technical analysis.
category_characteristics.Software: This user is strongly value-oriented, quickly highlighting appealing cost factors like free content and then testing products for sustained functionality. Their writing is brief and emotionally expressive—opening with enthusiastic praise before noting durability flaws—showing a pragmatic desire to alert others to both initial excitement and potential long-term issues.

AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.product_description: Corel CorelDRAW Home and Student Suite X7 High-resolution and multi-display support View larger Corel PHOTO-PAINT Effects View larger Discover a world of new design possibilities for Home and school Design your way with CorelDRAW Home & Student Suite X7. Whether you dabble in design or have existing expertise, you can express your unique creativity by using the intuitive graphic design, illustration, page layout, web graphics and photo-editing features. Create eye-catching projects with a variety of content, including professionally-designed templates, high-quality photos and clipart. It's easy to edit photos and design reports, drawings, flyers, ads, business cards, logos and more. Plus, this latest version offers a redesigned workspace for ease of use, all-new special effects, learning videos, Quick Start Guide, and the ability to share content with other CorelDRAW users. Everything you need in one complete graphic design suite. All-in-one graphic design Create all types of graphics and photo projects with an array of intuitive and user-friendly features for designers of all levels. With graphic design, vector illustration, tracing, page layout, and photo-editing features, you'll have all the tools you need to design projects like a professional. Versatile drawing tools Produce original and compelling designs for print or web with a robust toolbox of text, graphics, layout and editing tools that help you get the job done. Whether you're designing reports, drawings, flyers, logos or flyers, we've got you covered. Advanced photo editing Enhance and improve any photograph with the professional photo-editing tools and effects included in Corel PHOTO-PAINT Home & Student X7. Popular photo tricks such as retouching and enhancement, bitmap creation, digital painting, and streamlined web exporting are just a click away. System Requirements: Microsoft Windows 8/8.1 or Windows 7 (32-bit or 64-bit editions), with latest service packs installed Microsoft Windows 8/8.1 or Windows 7 (32-bit or 64-bit editions), with latest service packs installed Intel Core 2 Duo or AMD Athlon 64 Intel Core 2 Duo or AMD Athlon 64 2 GB RAM 2 GB RAM 1 GB hard disk space 1 GB hard disk space Mouse, tablet or multi-touch screen Mouse, tablet or multi-touch screen 1280 x 768 or higher monitor resolution 1280 x 768 or higher monitor resolution DVD drive required for box installation (digital download included) DVD drive required for box installation (digital download included) Microsoft Internet Explorer 8 or higher Microsoft Internet Explorer 8 or higher Internet connection required for CorelDRAW Standard Membership, performance and stability updates, online content and Content Exchange Internet connection required for CorelDRAW Standard Membership, performance and stability updates, online content and Content Exchange Content*: 1,300 high-quality clipart and digital images; 100 royalty-free, high-resolution digital photos; 125 fonts; 75 photo frames; Over 100 professionally designed templates, including certificates, ads, business cards, letterhead, flyers, posters and more; 100 fills; Online Quick Start Guide; Over 2 hours of training videos; *CorelDRAW Standard Membership account required to download Comparison Matrix View larger CorelDRAW Home & Student Suite X7 View larger CorelDRAW Graphics Suite X7 CorelDRAW X7 (Home & Student version excludes Pro Features) ✓✓ Corel PHOTO-PAINT X7 (Home & Student version excludes Pro Features) ✓✓ Corel PowerTRACE X7 ✓✓ Corel Website Creator ✓ Corel CAPTURE X7 ✓✓ Corel CONNECT ✓✓ Barcode Wizard ✓✓ PhotoZoom Pro 3 ✓
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.review_text: Despite what the description says, it's not really suitable for those who are just dabbling.  The user interface has a ton of menu options and isn't quite as intuitive as I'd like for the novice/dabbler.  However, at this price for 3 users you get both an advanced photo editor and a publisher style document creator, so it is a pretty good bang for your buck.  The only thing I didn't like was that fact that it nags you at every startup to create an account in order to use online templates and objects.  I already have too many online accounts and really did not want to create another.
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.user_history_review:
For the money, this is a pretty solid "all in one" vector + photo editing suite, and it's a nice alternative if you don't want to pay ongoing subscription pricing. That said, don't buy this thinking it's going to feel simple just because it says Home & Student. The interface is still dense, with a lot of menus/panels, and if you're a first-time editor you're going to spend time hunting for things.

Once you get past that, the toolset is legitimately capable for flyers, logos, basic page layout, and cleaning up photos in PHOTO-PAINT. The templates/fonts/clipart are handy, but I'm not a fan of needing an online membership/account just to download some of the included content/updates. I want software I bought to work without extra hoops. Overall good value if you can handle the learning curve.
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.best_prediction_review:
For the money, this is a pretty solid "all in one" vector + photo editing suite, and it's a nice alternative if you don't want to pay ongoing subscription pricing. That said, don't buy this thinking it's going to feel simple just because it says Home & Student. The interface is still dense, with a lot of menus/panels, and if you're a first-time editor you're going to spend time hunting for things.

Once you get past that, the toolset is legitimately capable for flyers, logos, basic page layout, and cleaning up photos in PHOTO-PAINT. The templates/fonts/clipart are handy, but I'm not a fan of needing an online membership/account just to download some of the included content/updates. I want software I bought to work without extra hoops. Overall good value if you can handle the learning curve.
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.user_norm_context:
- Begin with the strong value proposition for cost vs. features, then caution that the interface is dense and may overwhelm hobbyists or first-time editors.
- Detail specific signs of a steep learning curve (crowded menus, numerous configuration panels) so novices can gauge preparedness.
- Encourage more experienced users by noting the rich toolset, but clearly separate expectations for dabblers versus seasoned creators.

leave_one_out_history_reviews.review_text: I have been using turbo tax every year for at least the last 10 years, except for the one year I gave H&R Block a try.  I ditched the  H&R Block and never used it again because it made it way to easy to miss deductions and to find out what you had missed.  There are a lot of things I love and hate about turbo tax, but mostly I like it.  The software interface and navigation is annoying, sometimes if I revisit a section it makes me answer the same questions again instead of remember that I had already answered all of that.  But what I like, is that I can dig down into the nitty gritty and see and compare literally everything about my taxes, as well as hop back and forth between sections if I want to (I tend to jump from topic to topic in the beginning to get the gist of whether I owe or not, then go back and revisit all the details of each topic).  The reports, comparisons, and online help are what really sets it apart. If there's a topic or dediction that I don't understand the rules of I can click help and immediately get a detailed explanationi of what it is.  So, as much as it has it's annoyances, I really can't imagine doing my taxes without it and will continue to purchase it year after year.

it's great to have the full featured pro Acrobat for creating PDF's, except for some stupid reason they decided to remove the option to flatten layers.  So if you are putting in a stamp on a document, without flattening it, anyone can lift your stamp and use it elsewhere.  I read on the internet that some have gotten around this by using a javascript to put that menu option back, but I was unable to get it to work and got too frustrated with the whole idea of having to add back a feature to such expensive software that i just quit using it.  And honestly, at this price, why it should be missing any essential features is beyond me.  I have a much cheaper Foxit PDF creator that does this just fine.  I'm also not a fan of the new menu layouts - its a huge learning curve compared to older versions and it seems like even after you hunt around and find the menu item you want, it requires multiple steps  to actually navigte to the menu item.  Overall the user interface feels very immature and unintuitive.

Love how easy it makes it to do my taxes, as well as being able to dig down into reports to see year by year comparisons. Hate that it always wants you to give them your personal info every time you open it so they can mail you marketing materials in the future. I mean really, I already bought their software, do they really need to send me more ads and stuff? I also wish they could find a way to streamline their code so that it would run a little quicker. I have a fairly new laptop with a ssd drive and it still runs pretty slow. But, since I haven't yet found a better alternative, I continue to use it year after year. I did try the H&R block tax cut one year, but it almost missed a key deduction and it was hard to go thru and compare to see what deduction was missing so I haven't used it again.
If you are unsure what version you need, you can always buy the basic version and updgrade from within the software if you find you need the deluxe or home and small business version.

AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.product_description: It's Fibbage - the lying, bluffing, fib-till-you-win multiplayer trivia party game from the makers of YOU DON'T KNOW JACK! Fool your friends with your lies, avoid theirs, and find the (outrageous) truth. And get this: your phone or tablet is your controller! It's easy. Just type in your lies to questions like: "The mayor of Rabbit Hash, Kentucky is a _____." Baby? Rabbit? Ham sandwich? It's up to you! Fool your opponents into thinking your lie is true and you'll rack up the points! But be careful, if you pick an opponent's lie, they'll be doing a Fibbage fancy dance. There's a free version that features a handful of starter questions, and you can upgrade to full Fibbage to get hours and hours of game party material! So grab your most trusting friends, family, and any other easily-fooled nitwits and get ready to laugh till you can't lie no more. Think your B.S. don't stink? You're about to find out! Product feature bullets • Play with 2-8 players!• Use your phone or tablet - or any device with Internet access - to join and play.• Quick 7-question games (but you'll just keep playing over and over - it's what everybody does).• Specialty questions like Celebrity Tweets and Road Trip! And of course...The Final Fibbage.• Free version upgradable to full version with hundreds more outrageous questions.
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.review_text: This is a must have for family game night on your fireTv.  It is so much fun, and the fact that everyone can use their phone as a controller is just pure genius.
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.user_history_review: This is still one of my go-to "everyone jump in" games on Fire TV because friends/family can join instantly from their phones as controllers, and it turns into a loud, fun couch-co-op night fast. The lie-typing mechanic is the standout and the 7-question rounds keep it moving. Free pack is fine, but you'll want the upgrade pretty quick for variety.
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.best_prediction_review: This is still one of my go-to "everyone jump in" games on Fire TV because friends/family can join instantly from their phones as controllers, and it turns into a loud, fun couch-co-op night fast. The lie-typing mechanic is the standout and the 7-question rounds keep it moving. Free pack is fine, but you'll want the upgrade pretty quick for variety.
AGQ44DK7UC7TQMR4AK7I57IDMDSA.user_norm_context:
- Lead by celebrating how effortlessly friends and family join the game on Fire TV using their phones as controllers; label this mechanic a standout innovation.
- Emphasize the lively, couch-co-op atmosphere and suitability for family game night before touching on any other attributes.
- Mention question variety or potential upsells only in passing and only if they noticeably curb the immediate fun; otherwise keep focus on the seamless party experience.

AEB5R4AU3242FXZFI6LJWQMYHKXA.product_description: For ages 13 and up only. *Offer valid with the purchase of a new Meta Quest 2 device (128GB or 256GB) ("Qualifying Product") between August 1, 2022 12:01 AM PST- December 31, 2022 11:59 PM PST and while supplies last. User account ("Account") required. Offer Item (digital content): Beat Saber in the Meta Quest Store (approx. retail value $29.99). Additional downloadable content, including future music packs and other content, sold separately. To receive Offer Item, Qualifying Product must be first activated between August 1, 2022 12:01 AM PST - January 31, 2023 11:59 PM PST through an Account without Beat Saber entitlement previously enabled on such account ("Activation"). Upon Activation, you will receive a notification message via email, in your mobile app, and in VR with a link to redeem the Offer Item from the Meta Quest Store. There are no redemption codes in connection with this Offer. Offer Item must be redeemed within 14 days upon Activation. Once redeemed, Offer Item remains available for download indefinitely. Limit of one (1) Offer Item per Qualifying Product, regardless of the number of Accounts connected to such Qualifying Product. Qualifying Products first activated through an Account with Beat Saber entitlement previously enabled are ineligible to receive Offer Item. Not valid on prior orders or purchases. Offer is non-transferable, not for resale, and not valid for cash or cash equivalent. Offer may be canceled or modified at any time without notice. Void where prohibited or restricted.
AEB5R4AU3242FXZFI6LJWQMYHKXA.review_text:
This is the new Oculus Quest 2, which is basically exactly the same as the older Quest except that it comes with half the memory, 128Gb of inbuilt storage rather than 256Gb and is priced $100 cheaper than the 256Gb version.

The Quest 2 is the perfect entry level headset to find out whether or not you like the VR world. Some people don't like VR for various reasons including vertigo and the feeling of getting sick, neck pain as the headsets are fairly heavy on your head etc. Other people love it and spend hours on it.

For your money your will get the headset itself, a fairly comfortable elastic strap to attach it to your head, two controllers and a charging cable. Setup is fairly simple. You connect it to your phone using bluetooth and just follow the prompts. You will need a Facebook account to tie it to and this puts some people off (I understand why) but the majority of people already have a facebook account anyway.

The Quest 2 has 2 1832x1920 LCD screens. Not quite 4K but a higher resolution than HD. For the money this isn't bad, especially considering the higher resolution Rift S is double the price. The eye pieces are slightly adjustable to 3 different positions, dependent upon the size of your head and space between your eyes. One great thing about the LCD display is that you can adjust the frame rate. I have mine set to 72Hz which I feel is much smoother than default 60Hz setting and this makes me much less prone to feeling sick. There is also a 90Hz refresh rate however for PC gaming that is too high unless you have a seriously beefy graphics card. Note however that you do not need a PC to use this.

The headset also has 3D capable audio built directly into the headset. The sound quality is OK but nothing outstanding. As such a lot of people also wear headphones when using the Quest 2 and there is a built in 3.5mm audio port to hook your headphones into. Personally, the sound is good enough for me and I prefer using it without the added weight of a pair of headphones.

The headset is quite heavy and although the soft elastic strap that comes with it is adequate, I would highly recommend purchasing one of the Elite, hard backed straps that you can find on amazon. These do make a lot of difference in distributing the weight, making wearing the headset much more comfortable and you can wear it for longer.

The Quest 2 has hundreds of apps and games available for it, from watching netflix and shooting games to fitness and boxing games. Even games such as boxing do help you to get in shape as you quickly find yourself ducking and weaving as well as throwing punches as you get immersed in the gameplay. These games are stored in the 128Gb of memory in the headset and aren't that large so even with 128Gb you should have plenty of room for a number of games and apps.

You can also attach your headset to your PC and play VR games on your PC. To do this you will need to buy an Oculus cable. The official one comes in at $80 which is very pricey. You can find third party cables that do the exact same thing for much less. I love to play DCS and Flight Simulator 2020 on my PC with the Quest. The immersion takes it to a whole other level, however in order to do so you will also need a fairly beefy PC to handle the graphics. My PC is a couple of years old, so its not powerful enough to turn up all the textures in Flight Sim to Ultra, but it is good enough to play with reasonably high definition. Honestly this is the main use I have for the Quest 2. At somepoint I will probably upgrade to the Rift S, but a new PC is in order before that.

So if your interested in dabbling with VR or don't have the budget to buy the Rift S then I would highly recommend the Quest 2. This 128Gb version, coming in at just $300 is well worth the money and I very highly recommend it.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity
AEB5R4AU3242FXZFI6LJWQMYHKXA.user_history_review:
This listing is basically the Beat Saber promo tied to buying/activating a new Meta Quest 2, so just know what you're actually paying for. On the Quest 2 side, the key specs are still the same: 1832×1920 per eye at up to 120Hz, and the lenses are decent once you take the time to dial in IPD and strap fit. Clarity is good in the sweet spot for rhythm games like Beat Saber, but it's not "edge-to-edge" sharp and you can get some glare if your fit is off.

Comfort is the make-or-break thing for me. The stock strap is fine for short sessions, but for anything longer I ended up needing a better strap (even a cheap halo style) and a different face pad to reduce pressure on my cheeks/forehead. Once I did that, Beat Saber sessions were way more enjoyable and the headset felt less front-heavy.

PC link is still a huge value multiplier if you have a gaming PC. Wired Link works reliably with a good cable, and Air Link can be great if your Wi‑Fi is solid, but it's more sensitive to your network than people admit. Having PCVR access is what keeps the Quest 2 from feeling like a closed toy, even though you're still dealing with Meta's ecosystem.

The promo itself is straightforward (no code), but the fine print is strict: you need a Meta account, you have to activate in the window, and you only get it if your account didn't already own Beat Saber. Also, don't forget the "additional music packs sold separately" part-Beat Saber can turn into ongoing DLC spend if you want specific tracks.

Overall, if you were already buying a new Quest 2, getting Beat Saber included is solid value. If you're trying to buy this as "just the game," this isn't that, and the account/eligibility restrictions are the main reason I wouldn't recommend it blindly.
user_history_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity

- Lead with the headset's display resolution, refresh rate, and lens clarity, then cover processing power or storage as supporting points.

leave_one_out_history_reviews.review_text:
This is the new Oculus Quest 2, which is basically exactly the same as the older Quest except that it comes with half the memory, 128Gb of inbuilt storage rather than 256Gb and is priced $100 cheaper than the 256Gb version.

The Quest 2 is the perfect entry level headset to find out whether or not you like the VR world. Some people don't like VR for various reasons including vertigo and the feeling of getting sick, neck pain as the headsets are fairly heavy on your head etc. Other people love it and spend hours on it.

For your money your will get the headset itself, a fairly comfortable elastic strap to attach it to your head, two controllers and a charging cable. Setup is fairly simple. You connect it to your phone using bluetooth and just follow the prompts. You will need a Facebook account to tie it to and this puts some people off (I understand why) but the majority of people already have a facebook account anyway.

The Quest 2 has 2 1832x1920 LCD screens. Not quite 4K but a higher resolution than HD. For the money this isn't bad, especially considering the higher resolution Rift S is double the price. The eye pieces are slightly adjustable to 3 different positions, dependent upon the size of your head and space between your eyes. One great thing about the LCD display is that you can adjust the frame rate. I have mine set to 72Hz which I feel is much smoother than default 60Hz setting and this makes me much less prone to feeling sick. There is also a 90Hz refresh rate however for PC gaming that is too high unless you have a seriously beefy graphics card. Note however that you do not need a PC to use this.

The headset also has 3D capable audio built directly into the headset. The sound quality is OK but nothing outstanding. As such a lot of people also wear headphones when using the Quest 2 and there is a built in 3.5mm audio port to hook your headphones into. Personally, the sound is good enough for me and I prefer using it without the added weight of a pair of headphones.

The headset is quite heavy and although the soft elastic strap that comes with it is adequate, I would highly recommend purchasing one of the Elite, hard backed straps that you can find on amazon. These do make a lot of difference in distributing the weight, making wearing the headset much more comfortable and you can wear it for longer.

The Quest 2 has hundreds of apps and games available for it, from watching netflix and shooting games to fitness and boxing games. Even games such as boxing do help you to get in shape as you quickly find yourself ducking and weaving as well as throwing punches as you get immersed in the gameplay. These games are stored in the 128Gb of memory in the headset and aren't that large so even with 128Gb you should have plenty of room for a number of games and apps.

You can also attach your headset to your PC and play VR games on your PC. To do this you will need to buy an Oculus cable. The official one comes in at $80 which is very pricey. You can find third party cables that do the exact same thing for much less. I love to play DCS and Flight Simulator 2020 on my PC with the Quest. The immersion takes it to a whole other level, however in order to do so you will also need a fairly beefy PC to handle the graphics. My PC is a couple of years old, so its not powerful enough to turn up all the textures in Flight Sim to Ultra, but it is good enough to play with reasonably high definition. Honestly this is the main use I have for the Quest 2. At somepoint I will probably upgrade to the Rift S, but a new PC is in order before that.

So if your interested in dabbling with VR or don't have the budget to buy the Rift S then I would highly recommend the Quest 2. This 128Gb version, coming in at just $300 is well worth the money and I very highly recommend it.

AGCSHQRXX6N4TA2ZNC5BZHQ6PQYA.product_description: RIG 700HX Ultralight Wireless Gaming Headset with 3D Surround Sound for Xbox Series X|S, Xbox One, PC, USB
AGCSHQRXX6N4TA2ZNC5BZHQ6PQYA.review_text: If you have a big head, this headset isn't for you. Even put on the biggest size wasn't even close to fitting properly. The hyperX Flights fight perfectly, however.

leave_one_out_history_reviews.review_text: If you have a big head, this headset isn't for you. Even put on the biggest size wasn't even close to fitting properly. The hyperX Flights fight perfectly, however.

One of the best games that I’ve played in quite sometime. Pure chaos, 32v32 online medieval warfare. Lots of different classes and weapons to choose from as well.
The world is your battlefield.
Want to throw a chicken at someone? You can. Want to give someone splinters and smack them with a tree log? You can. Want to cut someone’s head off then smack their buddy with it? You can. Very diverse and wild gameplay.
At the moment it is kinda barebones, however. No stat section, theres crossplay but you can’t party up with crossplay friends(incredibly stupid), and while the maps are pretty incredible and diverse there’s only a handful right now. Most of these issues are coming soon, but who knows when that will be.

Great, addictive game otherwise.

I’ve been waiting for this game for a while, and the end result is just an average shooter.
I’ve put about 5 hours in so far, and nothing has blown me away or come off as a new addition to the genre.
Graphics are below average, and the colors are extremely muddy, even on the seriesX.
Gameplay is clunky and not very smooth.
Story is forgettable and generic.
Most annoying issues I have
#1, server issues. I’m sure it’ll get fixed eventually, but the fact you need a constant connection to the server to play by yourself is absurd.
#2, the enemies just shoot and shoot, reload and shoot more. There’s no strategy behind what they do.

Overall it’s a fine game, just don’t get your expectations up.

AGAKR2KZZS2OMN2GORTKQEW7PXPA.product_description: Experience a military shooter set in a diverse & hostile open world that you can play solo or up to four-player co-op.
AGAKR2KZZS2OMN2GORTKQEW7PXPA.review_text:
I believe this game code was written by kids in grade school. Hmmm, maybe not. They would have done a better job.

It's obvious poor code was lifted from Wildlands because the same bugs from that game (which should have been fixed considering how long it's been out) are still in this one. Very disappointing in so many ways.

The graphics look like games I bought 15 years ago.

The next game they release will only be purchased by me when it's in the clearance bin at the Dollar Store.
AGAKR2KZZS2OMN2GORTKQEW7PXPA.user_history_review:
Feels like the sequel that still ships with the same old instability and janky AI/pathing I dealt with in the last one. I'm getting random glitches mid-mission and it just screams copy-pasted code instead of a real step forward.

The open world is big and co-op can be fun, but the visuals look dated for a modern release and no discount or extra modes make up for core bugs that should've been fixed first. If they don't clean this up with real patches (and keep servers reliable), I'm only grabbing future entries at rock-bottom clearance.
AGAKR2KZZS2OMN2GORTKQEW7PXPA.best_prediction_review:

AG7XEWOPZU4ZHA5QTL65XEDXZNHA.product_description: Product description Step into the shoes of Buffy Summers, Sunnydale's hottest vampire slayer. Fight against fierce vampires using cool moves and awesome slayer weaponry. With advice from your watcher Giles, you'll destroy the most fearsome creatures of the night. Amazon.com Buffy the Vampire Slayer is the first third-person action-adventure game based on the international hit TV series. Players take on the role of Buffy Summers, a 17-year-old high-school student who must balance adolescent growing pains with the responsibility of being the Chosen One. As the slayer, Buffy eliminates vampires and other demonic creatures that threaten the world's existence. It's up to you to save the human race from being "drained" during this devilish time. Fortunately, Buffy is not alone. Her friends help her solve mysteries and watch her back. Fans of the series will recognize the voices of favorite characters including Angel, Xander, Willow, Cordelia, Spike, the Master, and Giles. Players fight a range of enemies including Spike, the Master, Drusilla, demons, zombies, and other supernatural creatures that threaten mankind. All of Buffy's favorite haunts are richly detailed: Sunnydale High School, the cemetery, the Bronze nightclub, and the library, which is the portal that sits atop the Hellmouth. Special supernatural effects such as morphing creatures, eldritch explosions, dynamic fogging, and arcane spell casting enhance the dark, mythical setting of the game. An intelligent combat system allows enemies to perform counterattacks and learn from character's fighting techniques.
AG7XEWOPZU4ZHA5QTL65XEDXZNHA.review_text: I am not a fan of the show.  I felt it was a bit to cheesy for my taste.  But the game is NO JOKE.  I played this game till I beat and then played it many times after that.  Very fun and the story line is actually decent!
AG7XEWOPZU4ZHA5QTL65XEDXZNHA.user_history_review: I'm not even a big Buffy fan and I honestly grabbed this just for a cheap action game. It won me over fast because the fighting actually has some depth and enemies don't just stand there. The difficulty feels fair and it's fun to replay levels trying different moves/weapons. Doesn't feel like you need to know the show to enjoy it.
AG7XEWOPZU4ZHA5QTL65XEDXZNHA.best_prediction_review: I'm not even a big Buffy fan and I honestly grabbed this just for a cheap action game. It won me over fast because the fighting actually has some depth and enemies don't just stand there. The difficulty feels fair and it's fun to replay levels trying different moves/weapons. Doesn't feel like you need to know the show to enjoy it.
